source_detection.rule: Treat filenames containing ChatHub or Chat Hub, case-insensitively, as ChatHub multi-LLM source candidates.
source_detection.raw_preservation: Store or retain exact source bytes under SHA-256 before parsing. When the raw upload remains in an authorised external file source, store its immutable pointer, measured hash and coordinates.
source_detection.wording_rule: Do not alter original uploaded framework, prompt, user-message or model-response wording. Store normalized requirements, corrections, mappings and synthesis as separate derived records with source pointers.

paid_subscription.recommended_use: ChatHub browser/web user interface for parallel model questioning and supported export or owner-upload workflows.
paid_subscription.credential_rule: Do not store browser session cookies or scrape authenticated sessions.

custom_chatbot.recommended_use: Expose an approved Jarvis OpenAI-compatible endpoint to ChatHub Custom Chatbot where supported.
custom_chatbot.gate: Requires a scoped endpoint, authentication design, rate limits, privacy review and sandbox proof.

server_api.rule: Do not infer that a paid ChatHub subscription includes a server-to-server API.

upload_and_export.limitations: Unknown or changed schemas are preserved raw and marked for parser review rather than discarded.

multi_llm_consolidation.preservation: Each response and user prompt remains verbatim with role, model, line coordinates, word count, hash and duplicate lineage.
multi_llm_consolidation.derived_output: One consolidated answer may be generated only as a separate artifact.
multi_llm_consolidation.truth_rule: Consensus is not proof; source and claim verification remain required.

cross_module_routing.rule: Route applicable requirements to existing repository and module owners; do not create duplicate competing frameworks.

security_and_legal_controls.policy: Raw text is preserved exactly, but execution remains subject to applicable law, platform terms, security controls and owner approvals.

open_loops: Validate future ChatHub export variants as separately hashed source tranches.
Validate ChatHub Custom Chatbot interoperability with a scoped Jarvis staging endpoint.
Connect parsed message and requirement records to the persistent Knowledge Fabric.
Perform per-module delta extraction and implementation through each existing repository owner.
Complete staging and production only after provider, credential, privacy, cost and owner-approval gates.
truth_boundary: The supplied ChatHub text file is measured, parsed and routed. Cross-module mapping does not prove that every mapped feature is implemented or deployed.
